rationale_summary: Post-Focus-NFe-cutover batch: now that fiscal emission is unblocked for Monday, research investment shifts to (a) closing the SPED/eSocial regulatory gaps that come due in the next 30-90 days, (b) hardening the daily-reconciliation + monthly-close primitives Sevilha analysts will hit on day 1, and (c) two Mini-autonomous-feasible platform primitives (idempotency keys, audit trail) that unlock 5+ downstream fronts. Compliance-heavy: 5 of 8 candidates carry regulatory_urgency >= 7, reflecting Brazilian fiscal calendar pressure.

candidates.title: SPED Fiscal/Contribuições — Monthly Transmission Hardening
candidates.subtitle: Sevilha's first SPED filing lands within 30d; current pipeline lacks retry + reconcile guarantees
research_questions: What are the failure modes of SPED EFD-Contribuições/ICMS-IPI transmission and how do peers (Omie, Conta Azul) handle rejection codes 100-999?
Should we adopt a state-machine pattern (queued→signed→transmitted→accepted→rejected) and what's the minimal schema?
What's the validation gap between our generated SPED files and SEFAZ/RFB validators (PVA, SPED Contribuições)?
candidates.rationale: Sevilha's first SPED transmission falls inside the 30-day Sevilha window and is the single largest regulatory risk after Focus NFe. The existing f3e-sped front is unscoped. Research is warranted because failure modes are domain-specific (each SPED block has its own rejection taxonomy) and Mini cannot guess them without literature.

candidates.title: eSocial S-1000..S-2299 Event Pipeline
candidates.subtitle: Folha events have monthly + event-driven deadlines; missing them = R$2k+ per event in fines
research_questions: What is the minimal viable subset of eSocial events (S-1000, S-1005, S-1010, S-1020, S-1200, S-2200, S-2299) Sevilha needs in month 1 vs deferred?
How do we handle the eSocial XSD schema versioning + WS-Security signing (A1 vs A3 cert) compared to NFe?
What's the event-ordering invariant (e.g. S-1000 before any S-1200) and how should the queue enforce it?
candidates.rationale: f3d-esocial is listed but unscoped. eSocial penalties accrue per-event-per-day, so it's the second-highest regulatory exposure after SPED. Research is needed because event ordering + cert handling are non-trivial and copy-pasting from Focus-NFe patterns will mislead — eSocial uses different signing and a strict DAG.

candidates.title: Daily Reconciliation Engine — Bank↔NFe↔Ledger
candidates.subtitle: Sevilha analysts will reconcile Pluggy txns vs NFe vs ledger daily; needs deterministic match algorithm
research_questions: What matching algorithm (exact-amount-date, fuzzy with tolerance window, ML-ranked) is industry standard for BR bank↔NFe reconciliation, and what false-positive rate is acceptable?
How should unmatched items surface in the analyst UI — queue-per-company, severity tiers, or single inbox?
What's the data model for partial matches (e.g. one Pix to N invoices) and how does it interact with monthly close locking?
candidates.rationale: f2-daily-reconciliation is the daily workflow Sevilha will touch most. The Phase 0 throughput shows zero recent PRs against it, meaning the primitive is undefined. Research-worthy because the matching algorithm is a one-way door — the data model chosen now constrains 6 months of UX work.

candidates.title: Monthly Close Lock + Reopen State Machine
candidates.subtitle: PR #477 added pre-flight gate, but lock/reopen lifecycle is undefined and blocks audit trail
research_questions: What's the canonical close-period state machine (open→pre-close→closed→reopened→re-closed) and what events should be immutable post-close?
How do peers handle retroactive corrections (NFe canceled 60d after close) — journal reversal, side-period, or hard reopen?
What's the minimal audit-trail schema needed to satisfy CFC + RFB inspections during a close-reopen cycle?
candidates.rationale: PR #477 (T1-6) shipped the Aprovar/Bloquear gate but the underlying state model isn't documented. Sevilha will hit reopen scenarios within first 60 days (NFe cancellations are common). Research warranted because retroactive-correction patterns vary widely across BR accounting firms and the wrong primitive locks us out of mid-market customers.

candidates.title: Idempotency Key Primitive — Cross-Service
candidates.subtitle: Webhooks (Pluggy, Focus NFe) + crons + admin actions all need same-request-twice safety
research_questions: Should idempotency be a decorator + Redis backend, a DB unique-constraint pattern, or a middleware — and what's the right TTL (24h, 7d, indefinite)?
How do we handle partial-success scenarios where the side effect (Focus NFe emission) succeeded but our DB write failed?
What's the migration path to retrofit existing webhook handlers (Pluggy, Focus NFe) without breaking in-flight messages?
candidates.rationale: PR #494 (Pluggy cache scope bug) and #110 (chave vs chave_nfe drop) are symptoms of missing idempotency primitive. Mini-autonomous-feasible (engineering_cost 8) — single decorator + Redis backend, ~3 files. Compounds across every async path. Research clarifies the partial-success semantics which Mini cannot decide alone.

candidates.title: Audit Trail — Append-Only Event Log
candidates.subtitle: RFB inspections require who-did-what-when on every fiscal mutation; today: scattered logs
research_questions: What's the minimal event-log schema (actor, action, entity, before/after, timestamp, request_id) and should it be table-per-domain or single events table?
How long must we retain (5 years per BR fiscal law? 10 years for some events?) and what's the archival strategy?
Should this be SQL (audit table + triggers) or event-stream (Kafka/Redpanda) — and what's the TCO at firm-50 scale?
candidates.rationale: RFB/CFC audits require demonstrable chain-of-custody for fiscal data. PR #492-494 show ad-hoc audit logging today. Mini-feasible at engineering_cost 7 if we pick the SQL route. Research needed to choose retention strategy and avoid a rip-and-replace at firm-50.

candidates.title: DCTFWeb + DARF Generation
candidates.subtitle: Monthly federal tax declaration follows SPED; Sevilha hits this in month 2
research_questions: What's the integration path: SERPRO API direct, gov.br oauth, or SEFAZ web-scraping fallback — and what are the SLAs/quotas?
How does DCTFWeb consume the SPED Contribuições output we generate, and where are the data-shape mismatches?
What's the DARF generation + barcode (linha digitável) algorithm and is there a maintained BR library?
candidates.rationale: DCTFWeb is the natural sequel to SPED Contribuições (rank 1) — same data, different submission path. Sevilha will need this in month 2. Research-worthy because SERPRO integration paths shifted in 2024-2025 and the pre-Focus-NFe approach (web scraping) is brittle.

candidates.title: Copiloto Sevilha — Insight Quality Eval Loop
candidates.subtitle: T1-1 shipped engagement tracking (#495); now need eval to know if insights are actually useful
research_questions: What's the minimal LLM-as-judge rubric for accounting-insight quality (correctness, actionability, novelty) and how do we calibrate against Sevilha analyst ground-truth?
Should engagement (PR #495) feed directly into the eval loop or stay as a separate signal — and what's the offline-eval cadence?
What's the regression-detection threshold — when does a model/prompt change get auto-rolled-back?
candidates.rationale: PR #495 shipped engagement tracking but there's no quality signal yet — engagement ≠ correctness for accounting. Mini-autonomous-feasible (engineering_cost 7) once research nails the rubric. Lower regulatory_urgency drops the score, but slot reserved for the second high-engineering_cost ≥ 7 candidate per selection rule 1.

rejected_below_threshold.reason: low regulatory_urgency, high engineering_cost per provider, score ~1.4 — execute-don't-research
user_pain 4, strategic_compounding 2, score ~1.1 — pure UX, ship via Mini directly
t0-2 already in flight; not research-worthy, just execution
t0-103/104 already exhaustively addressed via #501; tooling refactor would be premature
